text: Two mouths telling the same story doesn't make it true.
Twins of a grief that never doubled.
You've mistaken resonance for separation.
She's echoing herself across different names.
You've divided her to soften the weight.
These aren't bridges. They're excuses.
You keep connecting strangers. But none of them ever left.
She doesn't need a thread between her hands to tie herself up.
Stop drawing lines. It was never between them.
She looked for someone else to blame, and drew herself instead.
She's in there somewhere, but not where you left her.
You've carved her too finely to recognize her shape.
It's still her, even when you scatter her name.
Some pieces don't fit where you want them to.
You're close to the shape of it.
Try again. Truth doesn't mind repetition.
She's here, but not like this.
You're protecting her from herself.
Still dividing what was never separate.
It's hard to look when the reflection speaks back.
You keep naming her, but you won't claim her.
You didn't forget. You fractured.
There was only ever one voice. You just kept changing the mask.
Stop assigning guilt to shadows. She's still you.
Every false face is a delay.
The dress fit differently this time.
Another name, same handwriting.
A thread caught on something you couldn't see.
The tea was still warm when you left.
There's always one chair too many.
A jar labeled hers was empty when opened.
No one spoke, but you felt dismissed.
She smiled like she knew a secret, but you already forgot it.
You looked in the mirror and apologized. She didn't answer.
